YUAN-DBL-EWR-IRN-019  ·  ro-yuan-019

Yuan Double-Gourd Ewer with Underglaze Iron-Black Splashes

元代葫芦形铁斑釉执壶 — Yuándài Húlu Xíng Tiě Bān Yòu Zhíhú

Iron-Splash Glaze 铁斑釉 Yuan Dynasty · 1279–1368 CE Southern Kilns, China Double-Gourd Ewer · Húlu 葫芦 Underglaze Iron Oxide Dronova Method Applied

Object Description

This elegant ewer in the double-gourd form (húlu, 葫芦) represents a distinctive category of Yuan dynasty ceramic production in which vessel morphology and glaze decoration are equally charged with symbolic meaning. The bilobed body — two rounded spheres of diminishing size stacked vertically — references the ancient gourd form that in Chinese culture has long embodied longevity, prosperity, protection against malign forces, and the continuity of dynastic and family life. In the Yuan period (1279–1368), the húlu ewer gained wide currency in elite and Buddhist monastic settings as both a functional vessel and an auspicious object.

The vessel is completed by a tall neck rising from the upper lobe, an angled pouring spout joined to the shoulder of the lower lobe, and a gracefully arched strap handle terminating in a small applied loop at the junction with the neck — a construction detail typical of Yuan southern kiln production. The unglazed footring exposes a warm buff-orange stoneware body with scattered dark iron specks characteristic of high-fired southern ceramic pastes.

The glaze surface presents the defining aesthetic feature of this object: a pale whitish-ochre base glaze across which iron-black splashes are freely scattered. These splashes result from the deliberate application of iron oxide slip directly to the leather-hard body or unfired glaze surface before firing, producing dark, naturally diffused markings as the iron reacts and migrates within the molten glaze during the high-temperature kiln atmosphere. The blurred, organic contours of the spots — some with darker centres and lighter halo edges — confirm authentic kiln reaction rather than post-firing addition. The mottled effect creates a strikingly naturalistic surface reminiscent of stone, tortoiseshell, or animal pelt, reflecting the experimental decorative aesthetic of Yuan ceramic workshops.

Iconographic significance of the húlu form: The double gourd was among the Eight Daoist Immortals' attributes and a symbol of the alchemical arts. Its bilobed silhouette also evokes Heaven and Earth joined, making it a potent emblem of cosmological harmony. Comparable Yuan iron-splash húlu ewers are preserved in the Shanghai Museum and the Palace Museum, Beijing, where they are attributed to southern kiln traditions of Jiangxi and Fujian provinces.

Microscopic Analysis (GOM)

Оптическое исследование проводилось при увеличениях ×10, ×20, ×40 по Методу Дроновой в режимах отражённого, косого и тёмнопольного освещения. Приоритетные зоны: ядро железистого пятна на тулове и край глазури у основания.

Zone A — Железистое пятно, ядро

GOM · ×40
×40

Ядро пятна: кристаллические агломераты оксидов железа в стеклоподобной матрице. Диффузная граница с окружающей глазурью — признак реакции в расплаве. Газовые пузырьки округлые. Признаков вторичного нанесения нет.

При ×40 ядро железистого пятна демонстрирует кристаллические агломераты оксида железа, embedded в прозрачную стеклоподобную матрицу основной глазури. Диффузный, постепенно размывающийся переход к окружающей глазури подтверждает реакцию в расплаве при обжиге — а не нанесение после обжига. Этот микроскопический признак является главным диагностическим критерием подлинности юаньских железистых пятен по Методу Дроновой.

Zone B — Край глазури у основания

GOM · ×40
×40

Край глазури: естественное истончение и кристаллизация у границы с незаглазурованным черепком. Мелкозернистая поверхность базовой глазури. Кварцевые включения. Признаков реглазирования нет.

Край глазури у основания демонстрирует характерное для высокотемпературного обжига естественное истончение и кристаллизацию без признаков последующего реглазирования или добавления вторичного слоя. Мелкозернистая поверхность базовой глазури с редкими кварцевыми включениями соответствует южнокитайской традиции производства юаньской эпохи.

Diagnostic Interpretation

Объект оценивался по диагностической матрице Метода Дроновой для юаньских кувшинов с железистыми пятнами. Удовлетворяются все главные диагностические критерии.

Diagnostic Parameter Observed Reference Standard (Yuan / Southern Kilns) Assessment
Vessel form (húlu)Canonical double-gourd bilobed body, tall neck, loop handle terminalYuan húlu ewer proportions✓ Consistent
Base glaze colourPale whitish-ochre with iron-tinted zonesOff-white to ochre calcium-flux glaze✓ Consistent
Iron-black splashesFreely scattered, blurred contours, diffuse halosUnderglaze iron oxide reaction in melt✓ Diagnostic
Iron spot microstructureCrystalline iron oxide agglomerates, diffuse glaze boundaryIn-melt reaction — not post-firing addition✓ Diagnostic
Body (footring)Warm buff-orange stoneware, dark iron specksSouthern high-fired stoneware paste✓ Consistent
Footring treatmentUnglazed, wheel-trimmed, firing tracesUnglazed footring, natural firing evidence✓ Consistent
Glaze edge (base)Natural thinning and crystallisation, no secondary glaze layerSingle-firing glaze termination✓ Consistent
Handle constructionArched strap with applied loop terminal — slip-lutedSouthern Yuan ewer handle convention✓ Consistent

Diagnostic Checklist

  • Canonical Yuan húlu double-gourd form — bilobed body with tall neck and loop-terminal handle consistent with southern kiln production
  • Iron-black splashes with blurred, diffuse contours — confirm underglaze application and in-melt reaction during high-temperature firing
  • GOM Zone A: crystalline iron oxide agglomerates embedded in transparent glaze matrix — primary diagnostic criterion for authentic Yuan iron-splash decoration
  • Diffuse glaze-to-splash boundary at ×40 — physically impossible if pigment applied post-firing; confirms authentic kiln production
  • Warm buff-orange stoneware body with dark iron specks at footring — consistent with southern high-fired stoneware paste tradition
  • Natural glaze thinning and crystallisation at base edge — single-firing signature, no evidence of reglaze or secondary layer
  • Applied loop terminal on handle — constructional detail specific to Yuan southern kiln ewer production
  • No evidence of modern restoration, artificial ageing, post-firing pigment addition, or reglaze

Diagnostic Fingerprint

Authentication at a glance — полный диагностический портрет объекта.

Diagnostic MarkerAuthentic Yuan Example
FormDouble-gourd (húlu), loop handle
Base glazePale whitish-ochre, calcium-flux
Iron splashesUnderglaze, diffuse contours
GOM — iron spotCrystalline Fe agglomerates in melt
Diagnostic MarkerAuthentic Yuan Example
BodyBuff-orange stoneware, iron specks
FootringUnglazed, wheel-trimmed
Glaze edgeNatural thinning, single firing
Museum comparandaShanghai Museum, Palace Museum
Диффузная граница железистого пятна с окружающей глазурью при ×40 — единственный неопровержимый микроскопический признак подлинного юаньского декора железистыми пятнами. Этот признак не воспроизводится при постобжиговом нанесении пигмента. В данном образце он удовлетворён в обеих исследованных зонах.

Cross-References

ro-song-011 — Qingbai Ewer with Carved Peonies

Form comparison: Song qingbai ewer vs Yuan húlu ewer. Both are multi-component assembled ewers with loop or strap handles; glaze technology and decorative approach contrast fundamentally — transparent carved vs iron-splash opaque.

→ View ro-song-011

ro-yuan-017 — Yuan Black-Glazed Meiping with Dragon

Iron oxide in glaze comparison: both objects exploit iron oxide for decorative effect, but through different techniques — reserve-incised iron-black ground (ro-yuan-017) vs scattered iron-splash on pale base glaze (ro-yuan-019). GOM microstructure of iron zones compared.

→ View ro-yuan-017

ro-yuan-018 — Yuan Cizhou Headrest with Lion

Iron oxide decorative technique comparison: iron-brown pigment on white slip (Cizhou sgraffito) vs underglaze iron-black splash on pale glaze (this ewer). Both use iron oxide as primary chromatic agent; application method and firing response differ.

→ View ro-yuan-018
